title: AST fails to handle implicit wires properly
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.38+4 (git sha1 0b835f28c, gcc 13.2.1 -fPIC -O2)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux, Windows

### Reproduction Steps

```verilog
module gold(a, b);
	output wire [1:0] a;
	input wire [1:0] b;
	genvar i;
	for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin
		wire x;
		assign x = b[i];
		assign a[i] = x;
	end
endmodule

module gate(a, b);
	output wire [1:0] a;
	input wire [1:0] b;
	genvar i;
	for (i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin
		assign x = b[i];
		assign a[i] = x;
	end
endmodule
```
```tcl
read_verilog -sv genblk_wire.sv

select -assert-count 1 gate/genblk1[0].x
select -assert-count 1 gate/genblk1[1].x
select -assert-count 0 gate/genblk1[2].x

select -assert-count 1 gold/genblk1[0].x
select -assert-count 1 gold/genblk1[1].x
select -assert-count 0 gold/genblk1[2].x
```

### Expected Behavior

Both module should match.

### Actual Behavior

```

-- Executing script file `genblk_wire.ys' --

1. Executing Verilog-2005 frontend: genblk_wire.sv
Parsing SystemVerilog input from `genblk_wire.sv' to AST representation.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\gold'.
Generating RTLIL representation for module `\gate'.
genblk_wire.sv:17: Warning: Identifier `\x' is implicitly declared.
Successfully finished Verilog frontend.
ERROR: Assertion failed: selection contains 0 elements instead of the asserted 1: gate/genblk1[0].x
Selection contains:
```
